description: Any object used as an implement for a particular task can be 'tenoa'. The noun focuses on the user's practical relation to the object, so an ordinary thing can become a tool in context. 'keli' (device) instead draws attention to an object's arranged working parts.
articulatory_notes: Set the tongue tip at the upper teeth for dental 't' and release into mid-front 'e'. Return the tongue tip to the teeth for nasal 'n' before stressed rounded 'o', then keep the following open 'a' separate as the final hiatus syllable.
usage_notes: Instrumental 'roe' places a tool beside the verb that says what the user does with it. A tool's presence establishes neither skill nor permission; those relations have their own words and clauses.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A tool can be understood, maintained, shared, and adapted at the scale of the people who use it.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A familiar implement gathers knowledge in its grip, balance, edge, wear, and repair.

semantic_domains.activity: an implement selected for carrying out a task
semantic_domains.creation: an object used in making, repair, or material work
semantic_domains.physical: a tangible implement handled by a user
